question: Is the canvas divided into a 2×2 grid of four equal panels by thin black lines, with consistent spacing and 4% outer margins?
Are small sans-serif labels A, B, C, and D placed in the upper-left corner of their respective panels?
In Panel A, is there an acute triangle ABC (A at top, B bottom-left, C bottom-right) inscribed in a light gray circle?
In Panel A, are three medians drawn intersecting at G, and three altitudes drawn intersecting at H?
In Panel A, are the feet of the altitudes labeled D (on BC), E (on CA), F (on AB) with right-angle symbols marked at each foot?
In Panel B, is there a Cartesian coordinate system with light gray grid lines and axes having tick marks from -4 to 4?
In Panel B, is the parabola y=(x-1)^2-3 plotted as a blue curve and the line y=2x-1 as a black straight line?
In Panel B, are the two intersection points marked as P1 and P2 with hollow circles, and projected vertically to the x-axis with gray dashed lines?
In Panel B, is the enclosed region between the parabola and the line (from P1 to P2) filled with light gray 45° diagonal hatching?
In Panel C, are exactly three blue vectors drawn from the origin to points Z1 (30°), Z2 (150°), and Z3 (270°) on a unit circle?
In Panel C, are there angle arcs labeled ANG1, ANG2, ANG3 with directional arrows, and a gray dashed line mapping Z1 to Z1I labeled ROT90?
In Panel C, is there a counterclockwise directional arrow segment drawn on the circumference of the unit circle?
In Panel D (left half), is there an undirected graph with 6 nodes (V1-V6) arranged in a regular hexagon, connected by an outer ring and three diagonal chords (V1-V4, V2-V5, V3-V6)?
In Panel D (right half), is there a 6×6 adjacency matrix with headers V1 through V6?
In Panel D, is the matrix symmetric with 0s on the main diagonal, and does it accurately reflect the connections of the graph (1 for edges, 0 for no edges)?
Are the colors in the entire image strictly limited to black, gray, and blue on a white background?
Is the image style consistent with vector line art, having no shadows and no perspective effects?
Are all text labels (e.g., ANG1, ROT90, V1, P1) clearly legible, using only Latin letters/numerals, and free from garbled characters?
